title: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie

seo.title: How to Make Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie — Easy Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie. This easy Middle Eastern recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas for a truly flavorful meal.
longTailKeywords: how to make Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie
easy Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie recipe
authentic Middle Eastern Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie
lowCompetitionKeywords: Middle Eastern Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie Recipe
h2Headings: What is Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Middle Eastern Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie — a beloved Middle Eastern classic

description: Imagine the aroma of perfectly spiced chicken, slowly rotating to achieve tender, juicy meat with a beautifully crisp skin. This Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie brings the vibrant flavors of the Middle East right to your table, making for an unforgettable meal that is both satisfying and easy to prepare. It is a true taste of authentic Middle Eastern cuisine, perfect for family gatherings or a special weeknight dinner.

instructions.title: Prepare the Shawarma Marinade
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, ground turmeric, ground cinnamon, cayenne pepper, black pepper, and salt until a smooth paste forms. This creates the flavorful shawarma spice blend.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the whole chicken in a large bowl or a resealable bag. Generously rub the shawarma marinade all over the chicken, making sure to get under the skin as well. For best results, cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or preferably overnight, allowing the flavors to penetrate deeply.

instructions.title: Prepare for Rotisserie Cooking
instructions.text: Remove the chicken from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking to bring it closer to room temperature. Truss the chicken tightly with kitchen twine, securing the wings and legs close to the body. This ensures even cooking and prevents parts from flopping during rotation.

instructions.title: Set Up the Rotisserie
instructions.text: Preheat your grill or oven to medium-high heat (around 375-400°F or 190-200°C) with the rotisserie attachment in place. Skewer the trussed chicken onto the rotisserie spit, making sure it is centered and balanced. Secure with the rotisserie forks.

instructions.title: Rotisserie Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the rotisserie spit with the chicken onto the rotisserie motor. Place a drip pan underneath to catch juices. Cook for approximately 35-45 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) in the thickest part of the thigh, without touching the bone. The skin should be beautifully golden and crisp.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the rotisserie spit. Transfer it to a cutting board, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for 10-15 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist chicken. Carve the chicken and serve immediately.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, add a tablespoon of sumac or a pinch of ground cardamom to your marinade.
Trussing the chicken properly is crucial for even cooking on a rotisserie. Ensure wings and legs are tucked in tightly.
Serve your Chicken Shawarma with warm pita bread, a side of garlic sauce (Toum), pickled turnips, and a fresh salad like Fattoush or Tabbouleh.
article: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie is a beloved Middle Eastern Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
